behaviors.description: Conversation history is trimmed to the most recent 10 messages before the LLM call when history is too long.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: failure is at a module boundary (history manager/prompt builder -> LLM client payload), not just isolated list-slicing logic. REAL-INFRA: no real OS/network/subprocess is required; a stub LLM client is enough. MOCK-HIDE: mocking only the trim helper could miss wiring bugs in the outbound request payload. LLM-DEP: correctness does not depend on model judgment. MULTI-STEP: no multi-agentic sequence. ADJACENT-COUNTEREXAMPLE (for higher levels): no concrete System/Agentic/Workflow-only failure is needed to catch this behavior; an in-process integration test can catch the real boundary bug. REJECTION-GATE: rejected Agentic/Workflow because behavior text is about "history passed to the LLM is trimmed," not response quality or multi-model decisions.
behaviors.test_description: Wire conversation service + prompt builder + LLM client adapter with a test double client that records request payload. Feed >10 prior messages, execute response generation, assert payload contains exactly the newest 10 in correct order/roles.

behaviors.description: Generated response answers the customer’s question (not generic/off-topic).
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: core failure is model-output relevance, not just wrapper logic. REAL-INFRA: does not require OS/subprocess specifics. MOCK-HIDE: a stubbed model returning a canned good answer would always pass while real model can drift/off-topic. LLM-DEP: yes, correctness depends on real LLM output quality and non-determinism. MULTI-STEP: single model decision is sufficient; no required multi-agent pipeline. ADJACENT-COUNTEREXAMPLE: integration test with mocked LLM passes (returns on-topic fixture), but production model intermittently gives generic reply. REJECTION-GATE: rejected Workflow because behavior text specifies one generation outcome, not multiple agentic decisions in sequence.
behaviors.test_description: Run against real LLM on a fixed prompt set of support questions; score whether answers address asked intent (rubric or human-labeled oracle), and fail when off-topic/generic rate exceeds threshold.

behaviors.description: Tone re-check detects overly formal output for casual-tier users and triggers regeneration.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: detecting "too formal" is a judgment-quality failure at the model/tone-classification step, then orchestration trigger. REAL-INFRA: no special OS/subprocess requirement. MOCK-HIDE: mocked tone checker with deterministic labels would hide real misclassification. LLM-DEP: yes, depends on real model/tone-evaluator behavior. MULTI-STEP: regeneration is triggered after one key model judgment; minimum needed is agentic (not full workflow) for this behavior. ADJACENT-COUNTEREXAMPLE: integration with fake tone checker always flags formal text correctly, but real checker misses formal phrasing and regeneration never fires. REJECTION-GATE: rejected Workflow because behavior text focuses on "identifies ... and triggers regeneration," not validating end-to-end quality across multiple agentic decisions.
behaviors.test_description: Use real tone-check path with casual-tier scenarios containing intentionally formal drafts; verify detection occurs and regeneration path is invoked (e.g., second generation request emitted).

behaviors.description: User message, initial response, and any regenerations are persisted with correct timestamps.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: failure appears when service/orchestrator hands off to persistence layer (multi-module state propagation). REAL-INFRA: local DB/filesystem integration is sufficient; no real external network or OS primitive is required. MOCK-HIDE: repository mocks can miss transaction/order/timestamp mapping bugs. LLM-DEP: correctness is storage integrity, not model output quality. MULTI-STEP: may include multiple records, but not multiple agentic decisions as test oracle. ADJACENT-COUNTEREXAMPLE (for higher levels): no concrete System/Agentic/Workflow-only dependency is required; in-process integration with real test DB catches true failures. REJECTION-GATE: rejected Agentic because behavior text is "persisted ... with correct timestamps," which is infrastructure/data correctness.
behaviors.test_description: Run conversation flow with real persistence module against test DB, force a regeneration event, then assert all expected rows/events exist with correct linkage, ordering, and timestamp fields.

behaviors.description: If tone-check regeneration occurs, the second response is the one delivered; rejected draft is never shown.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: failure is orchestration/state handoff between generation, tone-check decision flag, and delivery layer. REAL-INFRA: can be caught without real OS/network by wiring modules in-process. MOCK-HIDE: unit tests on each component can pass while composed flow still leaks first draft due to ordering/state bug. LLM-DEP: behavior can be tested by forcing "tone check triggered" and fixed responses; model-quality judgment is not the oracle here. MULTI-STEP: sequence exists, but minimum failure boundary is orchestration logic, not multiple real agentic judgments. ADJACENT-COUNTEREXAMPLE (for higher levels): no concrete Agentic/Workflow-only requirement if trigger is forced true; integration can still expose draft-leak bug. REJECTION-GATE: rejected Agentic/Workflow because behavior text is specifically "when tone check triggers... second response replaces first," i.e., delivery-state correctness after trigger condition.
behaviors.test_description: Compose orchestrator + delivery adapter + persistence in test mode, inject first draft and regenerated draft with trigger=true, execute flow, assert only regenerated text is emitted to customer-facing channel/API and first draft is absent.
